description_long: This government building is a large, rectangular structure with a flat roof and a uniform, modernist facade. The exterior is clad in light gray stone or concrete panels, featuring vertical grooves and a grid-like pattern that adds texture to the otherwise monolithic appearance. The roof includes several raised sections and skylights, suggesting interior courtyards or atriums, and there are visible mechanical units or rooftop structures. Windows are minimal and arranged in a repetitive, narrow vertical fashion, contributing to the building's institutional and secure look. The ends of the building are solid, with only a few small openings, reinforcing its fortress-like quality. The overall impression is one of solidity, functionality, and understated authority, typical of mid-to-late 20th-century government architecture.
description: A large, rectangular government building with a flat roof, light gray stone facade, and minimal vertical windows, exuding a modernist, institutional appearance.
description_view_0: The long side of the building is visible, showing the repetitive vertical grooves and a flat roof with several raised sections and skylights.
description_view_1: The short end of the building is shown, highlighting the solid facade with minimal openings and the depth of the rooftop structures.
description_view_2: The opposite long side is visible, mirroring the vertical groove pattern and rooftop features seen in view 0.
description_view_3: The other short end is shown, similar to view 1, with a focus on the solid, windowless facade and rooftop details.
attribute_public_entrance_design: The main public entrance is understated, likely featuring a set of double doors integrated into the facade with minimal ornamentation and accessible via a flat, unobstructed approach.
